Use when correctness depends on behavior that should hold across many inputs or states, such as monotonicity, idempotency, conservation, or structural rules. Do not use when no meaningful invariant is known yet or when the real task is auditing existing coverage instead of writing the properties.
skills.compatibility: Designed for Codex or similar coding agents with repository file access and an interactive shell. Network access is optional and only needed when repository validation or referenced workflows require it.

section_summaries.Intent: Use invariant-oriented thinking to test broad behavior through properties rather than only through a short list of fixed examples.
section_summaries.Trigger boundary: a rule should hold across many inputs or states; examples alone feel too narrow
section_summaries.Inputs: target rule or domain truth; current examples or tests
section_summaries.Outputs: explicit invariants; property-oriented tests or checks
section_summaries.Procedure: identify what must remain true across many cases; separate strong invariants from weak hopes or vague expectations
section_summaries.Contracts: each property should express a real invariant, not a wish; the test should broaden coverage beyond a small handpicked set
section_summaries.Risks and anti-patterns: writing weak properties that prove almost nothing; confusing random data with meaningful coverage
section_summaries.Verification: confirm the property expresses a meaningful invariant; confirm the invariant broadens coverage beyond fixed examples
